libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required by default. For core-only tests, prefer MapPropertySource, InputStreamPropertySource, or built-in .properties/.props parsing. If a test deliberately exercises system-property behavior, set and clear the property inside the test.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Hoplite documentation commonly demonstrates YAML/JSON/TOML/HOCON config files, but those formats require separate hoplite-* modules and are not necessary to cover hoplite-core itself.
Using ConfigLoaderBuilder.default() may include default property sources; generated tests should keep inputs deterministic by explicitly adding controlled property sources when possible.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Hoplite core is usable without external services or setup: the resolved artifact brings Kotlin reflection/coroutines transitively and exposes core decoders, MapPropertySource, system/env/property sources, preprocessors, and a built-in Java properties parser. Meaningful coverage is reachable with the normal generated Kotlin test scaffold.
